L1.area: Content rendering and output sanitization layer

L2.description: A stored cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where a sanitization policy applied to user-supplied HTML content does not restrict URL schemes in hyperlinks. An attacker can craft a link with a dangerous URL scheme, and when the sanitized content is rendered in other users' browsers, the injected script executes.

L3.area: HTML sanitization policies for repository metadata rendering
L3.description: A stored cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where the HTML sanitization policy applied to repository descriptions does not restrict URL schemes in hyperlinks. An attacker can create a repository description containing a link with a dangerous URL scheme such as javascript:, and when the description is rendered, the injected script executes in other users' browsers.

ground_truth.reason: The HTML sanitization policy for repository descriptions uses bluemonday.NewPolicy() and allows href attributes on anchor elements without restricting URL schemes, enabling stored XSS via javascript: URLs.
